creator: d.j.w.marks+bugzilla@gmail.com
text:
Hey,

I am also seeing similar performance regressions in recent FreeBSD. I was testing network performance between VMs on top of KVM:

$ uname -a
Linux pippin 3.13.0-36-generic #63-Ubuntu SMP Wed Sep 3 21:30:07 UTC 2014 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

$ kvm -version
QEMU emulator version 2.0.0 (Debian 2.0.0+dfsg-2ubuntu1.5), Copyright (c) 2003-2008 Fabrice Bellard

The test host is an i7 laptop that's about 2 years old running Ubuntu 14LTS. All VMs configured to use 1 core and 1GB of memory. All VMs picked up their IPs from DHCP although I did some testing using static IPs and didn't see a difference.

Out-of-the-box 10.1<->10.1, iperf (TCP, defaults, 60 second test): 783Mbit/s.

As above, +LRO on the receiver: 7.13Gbit/s.

As above, sending from Linux host: 6.94Gbit/s.

Out-of-the-box 9.3-p2<->9.3-p2, +LRO: 9.18Gbit/s.

As above, sending from Linux host: 11.8Gbit/s.

Out-of-the-box 8.4-p16<->8.4-p16 (LRO was already enabled): 7.34Gbit/s.

As above, sending from the Linux host: 12.1Gbit/s.

For reference:

Out-of-the-box Ubuntu 14<->14: 14.6Gbit/s.

As above, sending from Linux host: 20.2Gbit/s.

I have not seen any of the errors described by Eric.

And the send performance, VM to host:

10.1 to Linux host: 11.3Gbit/s.

9.3-p2 to Linux host: 14.8Gbit/s.

8.4-p16 to Linux host: 11.3Gbit/s.

Ubuntu 14LTS to Linux host: 19.6Gbit/s.

Very happy to provide more diagnostics or test patches. Was disabling LRO after 8.4 deliberate? That makes a huge difference and I don't know whether everyone would find out that they expicitly need to turn it on.

Thanks
